requirement: System must technically allow automatic log generation; logging cannot be optional or manual
evidence: ProvenanceMiddleware is wired at the LangGraph callback layer so records emit without per-node opt-in; bundle_hash (SHA-256 of canonical JSON) provides evidentiary integrity for the assembled log
gap_notes: Non-bypassability is an integration discipline at the host application — the protocol cannot enforce that an operator did not strip the middleware before running. Treat as a deployment audit obligation.

requirement: Logs must identify situations that may result in risk (Art. 79(1)) or substantial modification
evidence: Per-step status/error and the bundle-level outcome record malfunctions, failed steps, and aborted runs — the events most relevant to identifying risk situations
gap_notes: Risk *classification* per Art. 79(1) (deciding which recorded events constitute reportable risk) remains application-layer logic; the protocol surfaces the events but does not tag them as risk

requirement: Logs must facilitate post-market monitoring (Art. 72) — drift detection across deployments
evidence: Every agent_step records both model_id and model_version; comparing across bundles enables drift detection
gap_notes: Tool invocations do not carry model_id/version (tools are not models) — handled via tool_name/tool_version instead; consistent with the field-purpose split

requirement: Logs must support operational monitoring by deployers (Art. 26(5)) — linkable per-pipeline-run records
evidence: All four record types carry pipeline_id + session_id; pipeline_bundle aggregates them in record order

requirement: Record start and end time of each use
evidence: Both fields ISO-8601 with timezone; pattern-enforced in schema; emitted by step_emitter and tool_emitter at node entry/exit
gap_notes: Conditional rule timestamp_end >= timestamp_start enforced at test layer, not schema layer (listed in Known Issues)

requirement: Record the reference database consulted
evidence: Field is required-present on both record types (nullable string, minLength 1 when populated); description ties it to RAG corpus version / API endpoint version
gap_notes: Schema does not enforce population when reference data is in fact consulted — substantive coverage depends on deployer instrumentation. Cannot be schema-enforced without out-of-band knowledge of whether the call used external data.

requirement: Record input data that produced a result
evidence: SHA-256 of canonical-JSON serialisation of the input; lowercase hex, pattern-enforced
gap_notes: Hash is integrity-checkable identity not raw payload — Act text says "record input data" which is satisfied by recording a verifiable identifier. Raw-payload retention is a separate (and conflicting with GDPR minimisation) decision.

requirement: Identify natural persons who verified results (per Art. 14(5))
evidence: reviewer_id is a required non-empty array of strings on every human_intervention record; min length 1 (≥2 for biometric pipelines per Art. 14(5))
gap_notes: Identity strength (display name vs. signed credential) is application-layer; the schema requires presence and uniqueness but does not bind to an authentication system

requirement: System must be designed to allow effective oversight — a human decision point must exist in the pipeline architecture
evidence: Presence of any human_intervention record in a bundle is evidence that a decision point was wired into the pipeline; the HITL context manager is the structural construct that materialises the design obligation
gap_notes: Schema cannot enforce that a given pipeline includes a HITL node — that is a deployer design choice. Absence of HITL records is interpretable as "no oversight design" only for pipelines where Art. 14 applies.

requirement: Oversight must prevent/minimise risks to health, safety, fundamental rights
evidence: action_type enum (approved/rejected/edited/escalated) makes the risk-acting decision explicit and machine-checkable; rejected and escalated are the risk-prevention paths

requirement: Oversight person must be able to understand capabilities/limitations and detect anomalies
evidence: model_id + model_version enable the reviewer to look up capability documentation at oversight time
gap_notes: Full satisfaction is a UI/disclosure obligation on the oversight interface — the protocol provides the identifiers but not the human-readable capability summary. Anomaly detection is upstream design, not a logged field.

requirement: Oversight person must be aware of automation bias risk
evidence: UI/UX design concern for the oversight interface, not a property the provenance schema can capture
gap_notes: Addressed in Chapter 4 PoC interface discussion; deliberately not a logged field

requirement: Oversight person must be able to correctly interpret the system's output
evidence: output_before_hash proves the reviewer saw the actual output before deciding; optional justification_hash anchors the reviewer's interpretation rationale to a verifiable artifact
gap_notes: justification_hash is optional by design — required-presence would force every reviewer to produce written rationale, which is heavier than the Act demands

requirement: Oversight person must be able to disregard, override, or reverse the output
evidence: action_type ∈ {rejected, edited} captures the override decision; output_after_hash captures the post-override state. Together they constitute the before/after evidence that is the HITL record's primary novelty

requirement: Oversight person must be able to intervene or halt via a stop mechanism
evidence: action_type: escalated represents the halt/escalation path; intervention_timestamp anchors the moment of intervention so chronology against subsequent records proves timeliness
gap_notes: Whether escalation actually halted the downstream pipeline is a host-application enforcement question; the protocol records the intent and timing

requirement: For biometric ID systems: no decision taken unless verified by at least two natural persons
evidence: reviewer_id is an array, accommodating multiple reviewers; uniqueItems prevents duplicate sign-off
gap_notes: Schema enforces minItems: 1 universally; the ≥2 constraint for biometric pipelines is not schema-encoded. Would require a deployment-profile schema variant or a pipeline-type discriminator to enforce mechanically.

requirement: Users must be informed they are interacting with an AI system (at first interaction)
evidence: Boolean field on pipeline_bundle; true substantiates the obligation, false records the obligation was not met (do-not-count semantics in reporting.obligations._field_present)
gap_notes: Only records whether disclosure happened — not when, where, or in what form. Tying disclosure_presented to evidence of the actual disclosure message is out of scope (artifact-level).

requirement: AI-generated content (audio/image/video/text) must be marked machine-readable as artificially generated
evidence: Marker belongs on the output artifact itself, not on the provenance record; output_hash links to the artifact where the marker should reside
gap_notes: Provenance protocol and artifact-level watermarking (C2PA, SynthID) are complementary; chapter 6 references this layered relationship

requirement: Deployers of emotion recognition / biometric categorisation systems must inform exposed persons
evidence: Upstream design/disclosure requirement directed at exposed third parties, not a property of the agent pipeline's internal record stream

requirement: Deployers generating/manipulating deepfake content must disclose it
evidence: User-facing disclosure obligation; output_hash on tool_invocation links to the deepfake artifact but the disclosure itself is application-layer

requirement: AI-generated text exempted from disclosure if reviewed with editorial responsibility
evidence: action_type: approved combined with reviewer_role anchored to "editor" (or equivalent role taxonomy) constitutes the editorial-review evidence that triggers the exemption
gap_notes: reviewer_role is free-form string by design — deployment-specific role taxonomies vary. "Equivalent to editor" is a judgement the auditor makes from the recorded role string.
